name: parsley

metadata.name: Parsley
metadata.summary: Parsing and pattern matching made easy.

documentation:
% -*- mode: rst -*-

# Parsley: A Pattern-Matching Language Based on OMeta and Python

You can read further docs at: <http://parsley.readthedocs.org/en/latest/>

## Summary

Parsley is a parsing library for people who find parsers scary or
annoying. I wrote it because I wanted to parse a programming language,
and tools like PLY or ANTLR or Bison were very hard to understand and
integrate into my Python code. Most parser generators are based on LL
or LR parsing algorithms that compile to big state machine
tables. It was like I had to wake up a different section of my brain
to understand or work on grammar rules.

Parsley, like pyparsing and ZestyParser, uses the PEG algorithm, so
each expression in the grammar rules works like a Python
expression. In particular, alternatives are evaluated in order, unlike
table-driven parsers such as yacc, bison or PLY.

Parsley is an implementation of OMeta, an object-oriented
pattern-matching language developed by Alessandro Warth at
<http://tinlizzie.org/ometa/> . For further reading, see Warth's PhD
thesis, which provides a detailed description of OMeta:
<http://www.vpri.org/pdf/tr2008003_experimenting.pdf>

## How It Works

Parsley compiles a grammar to a Python class, with the rules as methods. The
rules specify parsing expressions, which consume input and return values if
they succeed in matching.

### Basic syntax

`foo = ....`:

: Define a rule named foo.

`expr1 expr2`:

: Match expr1, and then match expr2 if it succeeds, returning the value of
  expr2. Like Python's `and`.

`expr1 | expr2`:

: Try to match `expr1` --- if it fails, match `expr2` instead. Like Python's
  `or`.

`expr*`:

: Match `expr` zero or more times, returning a list of matches.

`expr+`:

: Match `expr` one or more times, returning a list of matches.

`expr?`:

: Try to match `expr`. Returns `None` if it fails to match.

`expr{n, m}`:

: Match `expr` at least `n` times, and no more than `m` times.

`expr{n}`:

: Match `expr` `n` times exactly.

`~expr`:

: Negative lookahead. Fails if the next item in the input matches
  `expr`. Consumes no input.

`~~expr`:

: Positive lookahead. Fails if the next item in the input does *not*
  match `expr`. Consumes no input.

`ruleName` or `ruleName(arg1 arg2 etc)`:

: Call the rule `ruleName`, possibly with args.

`'x'`:

: Match the literal character 'x'.

`<expr>`:

: Returns the string consumed by matching `expr`. Good for tokenizing rules.

`expr:name`:

: Bind the result of expr to the local variable `name`.

`-> pythonExpression`:

: Evaluate the given Python expression and return its result. Can be
  used inside parentheses too!

`!(pythonExpression)`:

: Invoke a Python expression as an action.

`?(pythonExpression)`:

: Fail if the Python expression is false, Returns True otherwise.

Comments like Python comments are supported as well, starting with #
and extending to the end of the line.

## Interface

The starting point for defining a new grammar is
`parsley.makeGrammar(grammarSource, bindings)`, which takes a grammar
definition and a dict of variable bindings for its embedded
expressions and produces a Python class. Grammars can be subclassed as
usual, and makeGrammar can be called on these classes to override
rules and provide new ones. Grammar rules are exposed as methods.

## Example Usage

```
from parsley import makeGrammar
exampleGrammar = """
ones = '1' '1' -> 1
twos = '2' '2' -> 2
stuff = (ones | twos)+
"""
Example = makeGrammar(exampleGrammar, {})
g = Example("11221111")
result = g.stuff()
print result
```

→ `[1, 2, 1, 1]`

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_transportPassed
documentation: The sender is passed the transport recieved by the protocol.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_senderPassed
documentation: The sender is passed to the receiver.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_parserPassed
documentation: The parser is passed in the prepareParsing method.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_connectionEstablishes
documentation: prepareParsing is called on the receiver after connection establishment.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_basicParsing
documentation: Rules can be parsed multiple times for the same effect.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_parsingChunks
documentation: Any number of rules can be called from one dataRecived.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_ruleSwitching
documentation: The rule being parsed can specify the next rule to be parsed.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_ruleSwitchingWithChunks
documentation: Any number of rules can be called even during rule switching.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_rulesCannotBeSwitchedDuringParsing
documentation:
One can set a new rule during parsing, but it won't change the rule
currently being parsed.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_connectionLoss
documentation: The reason for connection loss is forwarded to the receiver.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_parseFailure
documentation:
Parse failures cause connection abortion with the parse error as the
reason.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_exceptionsRaisedFromReceiver
documentation:
Raising an exception from receiver methods called from the grammar
propagate to finishParsing.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_protocol.ParserProtocolTestCase.test_dataIgnoredAfterDisconnection
documentation: After connectionLost is called, all incoming data is ignored.

classes.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_anything
documentation:
L{OMetaBase.rule_anything} returns each item from the input
along with its position.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_exactly
documentation:
L{OMetaBase.rule_exactly} returns the requested item from the input
string along with its position, if it's there.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_exactly_multi
documentation:
L{OMetaBase.rule_exactly} returns the requested item from the input
string along with its position, if it's there.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_exactlyFail
documentation:
L{OMetaBase.rule_exactly} raises L{ParseError} when the requested item
doesn't match the input. The error contains info on what was expected
and the position.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_token
documentation:
L{OMetaBase.rule_token} matches all the characters in the given string
plus any preceding whitespace.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_tokenFailed
documentation:
On failure, L{OMetaBase.rule_token} produces an error indicating the
position where match failure occurred and the expected character.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_many
documentation:
L{OMetaBase.many} returns a list of parsed values and the error that
caused the end of the loop.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_or
documentation:
L{OMetaBase._or} returns the result of the first of its
arguments to succeed.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_orSimpleFailure
documentation:
When none of the alternatives passed to L{OMetaBase._or} succeed, the
one that got the furthest is returned.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_orFalseSuccess
documentation:
When a failing branch of L{OMetaBase._or} gets further than a
succeeding one, its error is returned instead of the success branch's.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_orErrorTie
documentation:
When branches of L{OMetaBase._or} produce errors that tie for rightmost
position, they are merged.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_notError
documentation:
When L{OMetaBase._not} fails, its error contains the current
input position and no error info.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_spaces
documentation: L{OMetaBase.rule_spaces} provides error information.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_predSuccess
documentation: L{OMetaBase.pred} returns True and empty error info on success.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_predFailure
documentation: L{OMetaBase.pred} returns True and empty error info on success.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_end
documentation:
L{OMetaBase.rule_end} matches the end of input and raises L{ParseError}
if input is left.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_label
documentation:
L{OMetaBase.label} returns a list of parsed values and the error that
caused the end of the loop.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_letter
documentation: L{OMetaBase.rule_letter} matches letters.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_letterOrDigit
documentation: L{OMetaBase.rule_letterOrDigit} matches alphanumerics.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_digit
documentation: L{OMetaBase.rule_digit} matches digits.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_runtime.RuntimeTests.test_listpattern
documentation: L{OMetaBase.rule_listpattern} matches contents of lists.
Tests for L{pymeta.runtime}.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_builder.PythonWriterTests.test_expr

methods.name: ometa.test.test_builder.PythonWriterTests.test_label
documentation: Test code generation for custom labels.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_builder.PythonWriterTests.test_markAsTree

Grammars containing list patterns are marked as taking
tree-shaped input rather than character streams.

documentation: Test generation of entire rules.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_tube.TrampolinedParserTestCase.test_dataNotFullyReceived

Since the initial rule inside the grammar is not matched, the receiver
shouldn't receive any byte.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_tube.TrampolinedParserTestCase.test_dataFullyReceived
documentation: The receiver should receive the data according to the grammar.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_tube.TrampolinedParserTestCase.test_bindings

Produce an object capable of parsing via this grammar.

@param grammar: A string containing an OMeta grammar.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_literals
documentation: Input matches can be made on literal characters.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_multipleRules
documentation: Grammars with more than one rule work properly.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_escapedLiterals
documentation: Input matches can be made on escaped literal characters.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_integers
documentation: Input matches can be made on literal integers.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_star
documentation: Input matches can be made on zero or more repetitions of a pattern.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_plus
documentation: Input matches can be made on one or more repetitions of a pattern.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_sequencing
documentation: Input matches can be made on a sequence of patterns.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_alternatives
documentation: Input matches can be made on one of a set of alternatives.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_optional
documentation: Subpatterns can be made optional.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_apply
documentation: Other productions can be invoked from within a production.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_negate
documentation: Input can be matched based on its failure to match a pattern.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_ruleValue
documentation:
Productions can specify a Python expression that provides the result
of the parse.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_ruleValueEscapeQuotes
documentation: Escaped quotes are handled properly in Python expressions.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_ruleValueEscapeSlashes
documentation: Escaped slashes are handled properly in Python expressions.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_lookahead
documentation: Doubled negation does lookahead.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_binding
documentation: The result of a parsing expression can be bound to a name.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_bindingAccess
documentation: Bound names in a rule can be accessed on the grammar's "locals" dict.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_predicate
documentation:
Python expressions can be used to determine the success or failure of a
parse.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_parens
documentation: Parens can be used to group subpatterns.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_action
documentation:
Python expressions can be run as actions with no effect on the result
of the parse.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_bindNameOnly
documentation:
A pattern consisting of only a bind name matches a single element and
binds it to that name.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_args
documentation: Productions can take arguments.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_patternMatch
documentation:
Productions can pattern-match on arguments.
Also, multiple definitions of a rule can be done in sequence.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_listpattern
documentation: Brackets can be used to match contents of lists.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_listpatternresult
documentation: The result of a list pattern is the entire list.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_recursion
documentation: Rules can call themselves.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_leftrecursion
documentation: Left-recursion is detected and compiled appropriately.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_characterVsSequence
documentation: Characters (in single-quotes) are not regarded as sequences.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_string
documentation: Strings in double quotes match string objects.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_argEscape
documentation: Regression test for bug #239344.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_comments
documentation: Comments in grammars are accepted and ignored.

methods.name: ometa.test.test_pymeta.OMeta1TestCase.test_accidental_bareword
documentation: Accidental barewords are treated as syntax errors in the grammar.
Tests of OMeta grammar compilation, with v1 syntax.

Resets the parser. The parser will begin parsing with the rule named

classes.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.receive
documentation: Feed data to the parser.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.end
documentation:
Close the input stream, indicating to the grammar that no more
input will arrive.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter._apply
documentation: # Implementation note: each method, instead of being a function
# returning a value, is a generator that will yield '_feed_me' an
# arbitrary number of times, then finally yield the value of the
# expression being evaluated.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter._eval
documentation: Dispatch to a parse_<term name> method for the given grammar expression.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.apply
documentation: Invoke a rule, optionally with arguments.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Exactly
documentation: Accept a one or more characters that equal the given spec.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Token
documentation: Consume leading whitespace then the given string.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_And
documentation:
Execute multiple subexpressions in order, returning the result
of the last one.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Or
documentation:
Execute multiple subexpressions, returning the result of the
first one that succeeds.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Many
documentation:
Execute an expression repeatedly until it fails to match,
collecting the results into a list. Implementation of '*'.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Many1
documentation:
Execute an expression one or more times, collecting the
results into a list. Implementation of '+'.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Repeat
documentation:
Execute an expression between C{min} and C{max} times,
collecting the results into a list. Implementation of '{}'.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Optional
documentation:
Execute an expression, returning None if it
fails. Implementation of '?'.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Not
documentation:
Execute an expression, returning True if it fails and failing
otherwise. Implementation of '~'.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Label
documentation: Execute an expression , if it fails apply the label to the exception.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Lookahead
documentation:
Execute an expression, then reset the input stream to the
position before execution. Implementation of '~~'.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Bind
documentation: Execute an expression and bind its result to the given name.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Predicate
documentation: Run a Python expression and fail if it returns False.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_Action
documentation: Run a Python expression, return its result.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.parse_ConsumedBy
documentation:
Run an expression. Return the literal contents of the input
stream it consumed.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.rule_anything
documentation: Match a single character.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.rule_letter
documentation: Match a single letter.

methods.name: ometa.interp.TrampolinedGrammarInterpreter.rule_digit
documentation: Match a digit.

An interpreter for OMeta grammars that processes input
incrementally.

a sender.

A dict of additional globals for the grammar rules.
```

A str from Twisted.
```

Stop parsing, since the connection has been lost.

```

A Failure instance from Twisted.
```

functions.name: parsley.makeGrammar
documentation:
Create a class from a Parsley grammar.

```
param source

A grammar, as a string.

param bindings

A mapping of variable names to objects.

param name

Name used for the generated class.

param unwrap

If True, return a parser class suitable for
subclassing. If False, return a wrapper with the
friendly API.

param extends

The superclass for the generated parser class.

param tracefunc

A 3-arg function which takes a fragment of grammar
source, the start/end indexes in the grammar of this
fragment, and a position in the input. Invoked for
terminals and rule applications.
```

functions.name: parsley.unwrapGrammar
documentation: Access the internal parser class for a Parsley grammar object.

functions.name: parsley.makeProtocol
documentation:
Create a Twisted `Protocol` factory from a Parsley grammar.

```
param source

A grammar, as a string.

param senderFactory

A one-argument callable that takes a twisted
Transport and returns a .

param receiverFactory

A one-argument callable that takes the sender
returned by the senderFactory and returns a .

param bindings

A mapping of variable names to objects which will be
accessible from python code in the grammar.

param name

The name used for the generated grammar class.

returns

A nullary callable which will return an instance of
.
```

functions.name: parsley.stack
documentation:
Stack some senders or receivers for ease of wrapping.

`stack(x, y, z)` will return a factory usable as a sender or receiver
factory which will, when called with a transport or sender as an argument,
return `x(y(z(argument)))`.

classes.name: parsley._GrammarWrapper

methods.name: parsley._GrammarWrapper.__getattr__
documentation:
Return a function that will instantiate a grammar and invoke the named
rule.
\:param name: Rule name.

A wrapper for Parsley grammar instances.

To invoke a Parsley rule, invoke a method with that name -- this
turns x(input).foo() calls into grammar.apply("foo") calls.
